title: MMR ETHUSDT 1h extended-maxBarsHeld CLOSED: NO_EDGE_DETECTED — signal structurally weak, not a timing issue
content: Null-screen for hypothesis 958735fb (MMR ETHUSDT 1h, maxBarsHeld=[24-96]) returned NO_EDGE_DETECTED. All 8 draws: PF=0.517-0.656 (mean=0.605, P95=0.655). maxBarsHeld ranged 25-85 bars (25h-85h) across draws — the extension was fully tested. Even at maxBarsHeld=81-85 bars (3.4 days), PF remained 0.61-0.66. Trade frequency adequate (n=215-248). REVISED DIAGNOSIS: The prior hypothesis (maxBarsHeld=12 too short) was WRONG. The losses are not caused by premature force-exit — they persist even with 85h of holding time. The MMR signal on ETH 1h (close < EMA200 - mult*ATR + RSI < threshold) is generating entries in downtrends that continue downward rather than reverting. The core signal is structurally weak on the 1h timeframe: ETH 1h momentum displacements from EMA200 are more likely to persist (trend continuation) than to revert. PIVOT: MMR ETHUSDT 4h (hypothesis aff00b73, approved). At 4h, each entry represents a multi-day displacement — more likely to be a genuine overshoot vs 1h intraday displacement. The 4h RSI also smooths out intraday noise. The lesson: on crypto 1h, the EMA200-extreme signal captures trend acceleration, not overshoots. On 4h, extreme displacements are more likely to be mean-reverting.

structured_data.revised_diagnosis: Signal structurally weak on 1h; 1h displacements from EMA200 are trend acceleration, not overshoots. Extending maxBarsHeld doesn't help.
structured_data.pivot: MMR ETHUSDT 4h (hypothesis aff00b73): 4h bars represent multi-day displacements more likely to be genuine overshoots
